intitule: Exercise 1: Reading Comprehension - Managing Appliance Problems

enonce.texte:
Read the following dialogue carefully and answer the questions.

DIALOGUE: A BROKEN WASHING MACHINE

Customer: "Hello, is this the appliance repair service?"
Receptionist: "Yes, it is. How can I help you?"
Customer: "I'm having trouble with my washing machine. It won't start.
Receptionist: "Could you please tell me the model of your machine?"
Customer: "It's a CleanPro 2000."
Receptionist: "Would you please check if it is properly plugged in?"
Customer: "Yes, I already checked that. It's plugged in."
Receptionist: "Hold on, please. Let me check your file. ... Thank you for waiting. When did you buy it?"
Customer: "I bought it about two years ago."
Receptionist: "Could you describe the problem in more detail?"
Customer: "When I press the start button, nothing happens. There is no sound."
Receptionist: "Would you like to schedule a repair appointment?"
Customer: "Yes, please. I need it fixed as soon as possible."
Receptionist: "A technician will come tomorrow morning. Please wait at home."
Customer: "Thank you very much. I appreciate your help."

questions.texte: What is the problem with the customer's washing machine?
questions.reponse: The washing machine won't start. When the customer presses the start button, nothing happens and there is no sound.

questions.texte: List two polite expressions used by the receptionist in the dialogue.
questions.reponse: Two polite expressions used by the receptionist are: 1) "Could you please tell me..." and 2) "Would you please check..."

questions.texte: What information does the receptionist ask for?
questions.reponse: The receptionist asks for: 1) The model of the machine (CleanPro 2000), 2) When the customer bought it (about two years ago), and 3) A more detailed description of the problem.

intitule: Exercise 2: Polite Expressions on the Phone

enonce.texte: Match the polite expressions with their correct usage. Draw lines to connect them.

questions.texte:
Match:
1. "Could you please..."
2. "Would you please..."
3. "Hold on, please."
4. "Thank you for waiting."

Usages:
A. Asking for a favour politely
B. Asking someone to wait
C. Thanking someone for their patience
D. Making a polite request
questions.reponse:
1-A (or 1-D), 2-A (or 2-D), 3-B, 4-C

Explication: "Could you please" et "Would you please" sont utilisés pour faire des demandes polies. "Hold on, please" est utilisé pour demander à quelqu'un d'attendre. "Thank you for waiting" est une expression de remerciement pour la patience de l'interlocuteur.

enonce.texte:
Create a short dialogue (6-8 exchanges) between a customer and a repair technician about a broken appliance.

Your dialogue should include:

  • A greeting and introduction of the problem
  • At least 3 polite expressions (could you, would you, hold on, please, etc.)
  • A description of the appliance problem
  • A solution or appointment arrangement

questions.texte: Write a dialogue between a customer and a repair technician.
questions.reponse:
Example Dialogue:

Customer: "Good morning. I need help with my refrigerator."
Technician: "Good morning. Could you please tell me what the problem is?"
Customer: "It's not cooling properly. The food inside is warm."
Technician: "Would you please check if the door is closing properly?"
Customer: "Yes, I already checked that. It closes well."
Technician: "Hold on, please, while I check my schedule. ... Thank you for waiting. I can come tomorrow morning."
Customer: "That would be perfect. Thank you very much."
Technician: "You're welcome. Could you please be at home between 9 and 11 AM?"
Customer: "Yes, I will be there. Thank you."

intitule: Exercise 4: Comprehension - Asking for and Obtaining Information

enonce.texte:
Read the situation and answer the questions.

Situation: You have a problem with your television. The screen is blank and there is no sound. You call the repair service to ask for help.

questions.texte: Write three questions you would ask the repair service about your television problem.
questions.reponse:
Example questions:

  1. "Could you please help me fix my television?"
  2. "Would you please send a technician to my house?"
  3. "How much will the repair cost?"

questions.texte: Write three pieces of information you would give to the repair service.
questions.reponse:
Example information:

  1. "The television is a Samsung LED 45 inches."
  2. "I bought it three years ago."
  3. "The screen is completely blank and there is no sound."

enonce.texte: You are a technician. A customer calls you about a broken microwave. Complete the dialogue with appropriate responses.

questions.texte:
Customer: "Hello, I have a problem with my microwave. It doesn't heat food anymore."
You (Technician): (1) "_____________________________________________"
Customer: "It's a LG Microwave, model 3000."
You: (2) "_____________________________________________"
Customer: "I bought it about 18 months ago."
You: (3) "_____________________________________________"
Customer: "Yes, I already checked the power cable. It's plugged in properly."
You: (4) "_____________________________________________"
Customer: "Yes, I can bring it to your shop tomorrow."
You: (5) "_____________________________________________"
questions.reponse:
Possible answers:

  1. "Could you please tell me the model of your microwave?"
  2. "Would you please tell me when you bought it?"
  3. "Could you please check if it is properly plugged in?"
  4. "Would you like to bring it to our shop for repair?"
  5. "Thank you. We will examine it and call you back."

questions.texte:
Appliance Problems:
A. Won't start
B. Screen is blank
C. Doesn't cool
D. No sound
E. Doesn't heat

Appliances:
1. Microwave
2. Television
3. Washing machine
4. Refrigerator
5. Radio

Match each problem to the correct appliance.
questions.reponse:
A-3, B-2 (or B-5), C-4, D-5 (or D-2), E-1

Explication:

  • Une machine à laver peut "not start" (ne pas démarrer).
  • Un téléviseur peut avoir "blank screen" (écran noir).
  • Un réfrigérateur peut "not cool" (ne pas refroidir).
  • Une radio peut have "no sound" (pas de son).
  • Un micro-ondes peut "not heat" (ne pas chauffer).

intitule: Exercise 7: Writing - Describing an Appliance Problem

enonce.texte:
Write a short paragraph (80-100 words) describing a problem you have had with an appliance and how you solved it.

Your paragraph should include:

  • The name of the appliance
  • A description of the problem
  • At least 2 polite expressions
  • How the problem was solved

questions.texte: Write a paragraph about an appliance problem.
questions.reponse:
Example Answer:

Last month, my laptop stopped working. I turned it on, but the screen remained black. I didn't know what to do. I called the repair service and said, "Could you please help me with my laptop?" The receptionist asked me to describe the problem. She said, "Would you please bring it to our shop?" I took it to the shop the next day. The technician told me that the battery was dead. They replaced it and my laptop started working again. I was so relieved! Now, I always take good care of my laptop.

intitule: Exercise 8: Dialogue Completion - Polite Phone Conversation

enonce.texte:
Complete the dialogue using polite expressions from the box.

Expressions: Could you, Hold on, Would you, Thank you, Could you please, Would you please

questions.texte:
Customer: "Hello, I need help with my air conditioner."
Receptionist: "(1) __________ tell me what the problem is?"
Customer: "It's not cooling the room properly."
Receptionist: "(2) __________, please, while I check the technician's schedule. ... (3) __________ for waiting."
Receptionist: "(4) __________ bring it to our shop for repair?"
Customer: "Yes, I can do that."
Receptionist: "(5) __________ please come with your receipt?"
Customer: "Yes, I will. (6) __________ for your help."
questions.reponse:
1. Could you
2. Hold on
3. Thank you
4. Would you
5. Could you please
6. Thank you

Explication: Les expressions polies sont essentielles dans les conversations téléphoniques. "Could you" et "Would you" sont utilisés pour faire des demandes. "Hold on" demande à quelqu'un d'attendre. "Thank you" exprime la gratitude.

intitule: Exercise 9: Giving Instructions - How to Manage Appliance Problems

enonce.texte:
Write step-by-step instructions (5-6 steps) on what to do when an appliance stops working.

Your instructions should include:

  • What to check first
  • How to describe the problem to a technician
  • What information to give
  • Use sequencing words (first, then, next, finally)

questions.texte: Write instructions on how to manage an appliance problem.
questions.reponse:
Example Instructions:

HOW TO HANDLE AN APPLIANCE PROBLEM

First, check if the appliance is properly plugged in. Sometimes, the problem is simply a loose connection.

Then, restart the appliance. Turn it off and on again to see if that solves the problem.

Next, read the user manual. It may contain solutions to common problems.

After that, if the problem continues, call a repair service. Use polite expressions like: "Could you please send a technician?"

Finally, give the technician clear information: the name of the appliance, the model, and a description of the problem.

intitule: Exercise 10: Creative Writing - Appliance Problem Story

enonce.texte:
Write a short story (150-180 words) about someone who faced a serious problem with an appliance and how they solved it.

Your story should include:

  • A clear setting and characters
  • A description of the appliance problem
  • At least 4 polite expressions
  • A resolution

questions.texte: Write a story about an appliance problem.
questions.reponse:
Example Story:

"The Day the Fridge Broke"

It was a hot Saturday afternoon. Mama was preparing a big meal for the family when she noticed that the fridge was not working. "Oh no!" she cried. "The food will spoil!"

I called the repair service immediately. "Hello," I said. "Could you please send a technician to our house? Our fridge has stopped working." The receptionist replied, "Hold on, please. Let me check the technician's schedule." After a few minutes, she said, "Thank you for waiting. A technician will come within two hours."

When the technician arrived, I said, "Could you please examine our fridge? It's not cooling at all." He checked the fridge and said, "The compressor is broken. I need to replace it." I said, "Would you please fix it as quickly as possible?" He said, "Yes, I will."

After one hour, the fridge was working again! We were so relieved. I told the technician, "Thank you very much for your help." He smiled and said, "You're welcome. Please call us if you need anything."
